modeling_requirements: OUTPUT CONTRACT: evaluate() must return a float present value. Return a non-empty JSON-serializable structure only when a caller explicitly expects structured output.

BLACK-SCHOLES FORWARD CONSISTENCY: Derive the forward from spot and discounting before calling Black-style kernels. With no dividends, F = S0 / discount_curve.discount(T); then PV = discount_curve.discount(T) * Black(F, K, sigma, T). This is valid under lognormal spot (GBM) with deterministic rates and European exercise only.

EQUITY MARKET DATA CONTRACT: Requires discounting and Black vol. Spot is an instrument/spec input, not a separate MarketState capability unless the product explicitly sources it from a live market snapshot.

GARMAN-KOHLHAGEN CONSISTENCY: Use spot FX, domestic discounting, foreign discounting, and Black vol. Covered interest parity gives F = S0 * df_foreign / df_domestic; then prefer the checked helper-backed FX vanilla path built on resolved inputs over reassembling Black76 basis claims inline, and use explicit basis-claim assembly only when the decomposition itself is the thing being validated. This is valid under lognormal FX spot with deterministic domestic and foreign rates.

FX MARKET DATA CONTRACT: Requires `market_state.discount` for domestic discounting, `market_state.forecast_curves[...]` for the selected foreign discount curve, `market_state.forward_curve` for bridged foreign-carry / routing context, `market_state.vol_surface` for Black vol, and `market_state.fx_rates` or `market_state.spot` for spot FX.

BLACK76 FORWARD-RATE CONSISTENCY: Each period prices independently as a Black76 call or put on the forward rate fixing at period start, discounted to settlement at period end. Valid under lognormal forward rate with deterministic discount factors.

RATE-STYLE SWAPTION HELPER CONTRACT: For a single-exercise European swaption, prefer the checked helper-backed path `resolve_swaption_black76_inputs(market_state, spec)` -> `ResolvedSwaptionBlack76Inputs` -> `price_swaption_black76_raw(resolved)` from `trellis.models.rate_style_swaption` instead of reassembling annuity, forward-swap-rate, expiry, and payment-count binding inline.
CAP/FLOOR LOOP CONTRACT: Keep the explicit payment-timeline loop for caplet/floorlet strips. Do not force the single-exercise swaption helper onto period-by-period cap/floor routes.
FORWARD-RATE MARKET DATA CONTRACT: Requires discount curve, forward curve (via forecast_forward_curve), and Black vol surface. Rate fixing dates and payment dates must match the instrument schedule.

JAMSHIDIAN ZCB OPTION CONSISTENCY: For European zero-coupon bond options under the Hull-White / Jamshidian contract, prefer the checked helper-backed path `resolve_zcb_option_hw_inputs(...)` -> `ResolvedJamshidianInputs` -> `zcb_option_hw_raw(...)`, or delegate straight to `price_zcb_option_jamshidian(...)` when the wrapper already matches the route. Do not degrade this route to generic Black76 on a forward bond price when the task explicitly asks for Jamshidian / Hull-White.

ZCB OPTION STRIKE NORMALIZATION: Normalize strike quotes to unit face before the closed-form kernel. Treat `63` on `100` face as `0.63`, and require the underlying bond maturity to remain strictly after option expiry.

ZCB OPTION MARKET DATA CONTRACT: Requires `market_state.discount` and `market_state.vol_surface`. Resolved inputs should carry discount factors at option expiry and bond maturity plus the mapped Black-style volatility quote at expiry.

CREDIT-SURVIVAL CONSISTENCY: For single-name CDS, price the premium leg and protection leg directly from credit_curve survival probabilities and hazard rates on an explicit payment schedule. Do not reinterpret CDS as nth-to-default, basket CDS, or first-to-default, and do not reuse Black76 call/put primitives.

CDS SPREAD UNITS: Running CDS spreads in task text are often quoted in basis points. Convert them to decimals before premium-leg accrual, for example 150 bp -> 0.015; do not multiply accrual by a raw value like 150.0.

CREDIT MARKET DATA CONTRACT: Requires credit_curve plus discount_curve. Declare both in the generated requirements set and keep the adapter thin around schedule generation, year_fraction, survival_probability, hazard_rate, and discounting.
DETERMINISTIC CASHFLOW CONTRACT: When all cashflows are known at pricing time and there is no optionality, PV = sum of cashflows * discount_factors. No vol surface or forward curve is needed.

operator_metadata.short_description: Fallback operator metadata derived from backend binding `trellis.models.rate_style_swaption.price_swaption_black76`.
